|last1=Swinford |first1=Steven |title=Boris Johnson denies aides warned him about garden party |url=https://www.thetimes.com/uk/politics/article/boris-johnson-denies-aides-warned-him-about-garden-party-7vjl2zs5b |access-date=17 January 2022 |work=[[The Times]] |date=17 January 2022 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20220117065253/https://www.thetimes.co.uk/article/boris-johnson-denies-aides-warned-him-about-garden-party-7vjl2zs5b |archive-date=17 January 2022 |url-status=live }}</ref> Johnson's spokesperson said that the report was not accurate, although other journalists were reported to have corroborated it.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Sparrow |first1=Andrew |title=No 10 denies reports Boris Johnson was warned in advance that party on 20 May 2020 was mistake |url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/live/2022/jan/17/uk-politics-boris-johnson-omicron-keir-starmer?page=with:block-61e56abb8f0856426bba1d19-pinned#block-61e56abb8f0856426bba1d19-pinned |access-date=17 January 2022 |work=[[The Guardian]] |date=17 January 2022 |archive-date=17 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220117151033/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/live/2022/jan/17/uk-politics-boris-johnson-omicron-keir-starmer?page=with:block-61e56abb8f0856426bba1d19-pinned#block-61e56abb8f0856426bba1d19-pinned |url-status=live }}</ref> On 17 January, Cummings reiterated his claim that Johnson knew in advance about the party, that Cummings raised concerns about it and that Johnson said it could go ahead, and consequently that Johnson had lied to Parliament about what happened. Cummings said he would "swear under oath this is what happened". [[Sky News]] reported a second source also said Johnson had been warned in advance about the party.<ref>{{Cite news |last=McGuinness |first=Alan |date=18 January 2022 |title=Boris Johnson 'lied to parliament' about Downing Street party, Dominic Cummings says |work=[[Sky News]] |url=https://news.sky.com/story/boris-johnson-lied-to-parliament-about-downing-street-party-dominic-cummings-says-12518606 |access-date=17 January 2022 |archive-date=17 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220117211230/https://news.sky.com/story/boris-johnson-lied-to-parliament-about-downing-street-party-dominic-cummings-says-12518606 |url-status=live }}</ref> [[BBC News Online]] reported that two other former Downing Street officials said that "they remembered Mr Cummings telling them that day he had warned the prime minister not to allow the drinks to go ahead".<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-60036435|title=PM who lied to Parliament would normally resign - Dominic Raab|date=18 January 2022|work=[[BBC News]]|access-date=18 January 2022|archive-date=18 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220118223257/https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-60036435|url-status=live}}</ref>
Starmer, a one time [[Director of Public Prosecutions]] said: "I think he broke the law. I think he's as good as admitted that he broke the law. (...) I think it's pretty obvious what's happened, this industrial-scale partying had been going on at Downing Street, not much of it is really denied, and I think that the public have made up their mind. I think the facts speak for themselves. I think the Prime Minister broke the law, I think he then lied about what had happened. (...) Once [[Sue Gray, Baroness Gray of Tottenham|Sue Gray]] has come to her findings, she will set out all the facts, she is very well respected, I think that all of those should be passed to the police to look at".<ref name="independent16/1/2022">{{Cite web |last=Stone |first=Jon |date=2022-01-16 |title=Keir Starmer says Boris Johnson 'broke the law' and 'lied about what happened' |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/politics/keir-starmer-boris-johnson-broke-law-lied-b1994086.html |access-date=2023-03-27 |website=The Independent |language=en |archive-date=4 May 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240504005226/https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/politics/keir-starmer-boris-johnson-broke-law-lied-b1994086.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
On 17 January, the ''Daily Mirror'' reported that Johnson attended a leaving party for Steve Higham, his former defence advisor, shortly before Christmas 2020 while strict coronavirus restrictions applied in London. Johnson was reported to have attended for a few minutes and to have given a speech.<ref name="Ind_b1994291">{{cite news |last1=Sabin |first1=Lamiat |title=Boris Johnson accused of attending leaving do and giving speech in December 2020 |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/politics/boris-johnson-leaving-party-downing-street-b1994291.html |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/archive/20220525/https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/politics/boris-johnson-leaving-party-downing-street-b1994291.html |archive-date=25 May 2022 |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |access-date=17 January 2022 |work=[[The Independent|Independent]] |date=17 January 2022}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last=Ponnezhath |first=Maria |date=17 January 2022 |title=British PM Johnson attended staffer's leaving party in Dec 2020 - The Mirror |work=[[Reuters]] |editor-last=Maxwell |editor-first=Kenneth |url=https://www.reuters.com/world/uk/british-pm-johnson-attended-staffers-leaving-party-dec-2020-mirror-2022-01-17/ |url-status=live |url-access=registration |access-date=17 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220117023610/https://www.reuters.com/world/uk/british-pm-johnson-attended-staffers-leaving-party-dec-2020-mirror-2022-01-17/ |archive-date=17 January 2022}}</ref>
On 18 January, ''The Guardian'' reported that Conservative rebel MPs had a plan to oust Johnson from office, named "Operation Rinka", after the dog that was shot in the [[Thorpe affair]].<ref>{{Cite news |last=Murray |first=Warren |date=18 January 2022 |title=Tuesday briefing: Rebel Tories deploy Operation Rinka |work=The Guardian |url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2022/jan/18/tuesday-briefing-rebel-tories |access-date=18 January 2022 |archive-date=18 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220118062922/https://www.theguardian.com/world/2022/jan/18/tuesday-briefing-rebel-tories |url-status=live }}</ref> On the same day, Johnson gave an interview to [[Beth Rigby]], after one of his immediate family had tested positive for COVID the previous week<ref>{{Cite news|last=Walker|first=Peter|date=13 January 2022|title=Boris Johnson unlikely to be seen in public for a week, says Downing St|work=The Guardian|url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/jan/13/boris-johnson-cancels-visit-to-vaccine-centre-as-relative-tests-positive-for-covid|access-date=28 January 2022|archive-date=28 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220128184916/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/jan/13/boris-johnson-cancels-visit-to-vaccine-centre-as-relative-tests-positive-for-covid|url-status=live}}</ref> and having not appeared in public himself for nearly a week.<ref name="sky_12519281">{{cite news |last1=Culbertson |first1=Alix |title=Downing Street parties: Is the game up? Boris Johnson looks defeated as mood turns sour in Tory party |url=https://news.sky.com/story/downing-street-parties-is-the-game-up-boris-johnson-looks-defeated-as-mood-turns-sour-in-tory-party-12519281 |access-date=28 January 2022 |work=Sky News |date=19 January 2022 |archive-date=27 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220127142448/https://news.sky.com/story/downing-street-parties-is-the-game-up-boris-johnson-looks-defeated-as-mood-turns-sour-in-tory-party-12519281 |url-status=live }}</ref> He repeatedly apologised, and said of the 20 May 2020 event: "I'm saying categorically that nobody told me, nobody said this was something that was against the rules". Sky News reported that one Conservative MP described Johnson in the interview as "absolutely beaten"<ref>{{Cite news |last=Culbertson |first=Alix |date=19 January 2022 |title=Boris Johnson says 'nobody told me' Number 10 lockdown garden party was against the rules |work=[[Sky News]] |url=https://news.sky.com/story/boris-johnson-says-nobody-told-me-no-10-lockdown-garden-party-was-against-the-rules-12519115 |access-date=19 January 2022 |archive-date=19 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220119200545/https://news.sky.com/story/boris-johnson-says-nobody-told-me-no-10-lockdown-garden-party-was-against-the-rules-12519115 |url-status=live }}</ref> and Rigby described him as looking "defeated".<ref name="sky_12519281" /> The ''[[I (newspaper)|i]]'' described the interview as "excruciating".<ref>{{Cite news |last=Merrick |first=Jane |date=19 January 2022 |title=What Boris Johnson said and what he really meant in excruciating 16-minute interview |work=INews |url=https://inews.co.uk/news/politics/what-boris-johnson-said-16-minute-interview-1409414 |access-date=19 January 2022 |archive-date=19 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220119174231/https://inews.co.uk/news/politics/what-boris-johnson-said-16-minute-interview-1409414 |url-status=live }}</ref>
Asked about Johnson saying he did not know it was a party, Starmer said: "The cover-up isn't worse than the crime, but the cover-up compounds the crime. Johnson's now on his third defence. His first defence when we tackled him on this at the beginning of December was: 'I've been assured there were no parties,' and his second defence when the video came out was: 'I'm furious there have been these parties; I've only just found out.' And if the third defence is true, then obviously the first two are false – and that's a major problem for him".<ref>{{Cite news |last=Hattenstone |first=Simon |date=2022-01-19 |title='Stop talking about the problem – fix the bloody thing!' Keir Starmer on Boris Johnson's parties and his plan to win power |language=en-GB |work=The Guardian |url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/jan/19/stop-talking-about-the-problem-fix-the-bloody-thing-keir-starmer-on-boris-johnsons-parties-and-his-plan-to-win-power |access-date=2023-03-27 |issn=0261-3077 |archive-date=19 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220119131440/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/jan/19/stop-talking-about-the-problem-fix-the-bloody-thing-keir-starmer-on-boris-johnsons-parties-and-his-plan-to-win-power |url-status=live }}</ref>
====From 24 January==== On 24 January, ITV News reported that a surprise birthday get-together was held for Johnson on 19 June 2020, allegedly organised by [[Carrie Symonds]]. Up to 30 people were said to have been present, including Johnson, Symonds and interior designer Lulu Lytle, who was working on Johnson and Symonds' flat at the time. It was said a [[Union Jack]] cake was served and attendees sang "Happy Birthday".<ref>{{Cite news |title='Union Jack cake' part of birthday celebration for Boris Johnson |last=Norris |first=Phil |work=WalesOnline |date=25 January 2022 |url=https://www.walesonline.co.uk/news/uk-news/union-jack-cake-part-boris-22861954 |access-date=10 February 2022 |archive-date=10 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220210201016/https://www.walesonline.co.uk/news/uk-news/union-jack-cake-part-boris-22861954 |url-status=live }}</ref> Downing Street said the prime minister attended for less than ten minutes.<ref name="auto12">{{Cite news|last=Brand|first=Paul|date=24 January 2022|title=Boris Johnson had birthday bash during lockdown, ITV News understands|url=https://www.itv.com/news/2022-01-24/boris-johnson-had-birthday-bash-during-lockdown-itv-news-understands|access-date=24 January 2022|work=ITV News|archive-date=24 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220124182412/https://www.itv.com/news/2022-01-24/boris-johnson-had-birthday-bash-during-lockdown-itv-news-understands|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="BBC_60121572">{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-60121572|title=Birthday celebration for PM sparks new party row|date=25 January 2022|work=BBC News|access-date=25 January 2022|archive-date=25 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220125090904/https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-60121572|url-status=live}}</ref> A spokesperson for Lytle's company said: "Lulu was not invited to any birthday celebrations for the Prime Minister as a guest. Lulu entered the Cabinet Room briefly as requested, while waiting to speak with the Prime Minister".<ref name="auto12" /> The Chancellor Rishi Sunak was reported to have been "unintentionally present" when the cake was served, while waiting for a meeting.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Blackall |first1=Molly |title=Minister claims Boris Johnson was 'ambushed with cake' at birthday party |url=https://inews.co.uk/news/downing-street-parties-boris-johnson-birthday-party-ambushed-with-cake-1423314 |access-date=26 January 2022 |work=i |date=25 January 2022 |archive-date=4 May 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240504005207/https://inews.co.uk/news/downing-street-parties-boris-johnson-birthday-party-ambushed-with-cake-1423314 |url-status=live }}</ref> ''The Guardian'' reported Downing Street sources saying that, in the evening, Johnson celebrated outside with family, as allowed under the then rules.<ref name="guardlive24">{{Cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/live/2022/jan/24/uk-politics-boris-johnson-conservatives-labour-nusrat-ghani|title=UK Covid live: Boris Johnson had birthday party at No 10 during 2020 lockdown, report claims|first1=Jedidajah|last1=Otte|first2=Andrew|last2=Sparrow|date=24 January 2022|work=The Guardian|access-date=24 January 2022|archive-date=24 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220124224201/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/live/2022/jan/24/uk-politics-boris-johnson-conservatives-labour-nusrat-ghani|url-status=live}}</ref> ITV News alleged that family friends then went up to Johnson's flat. A Number 10 spokesperson denied this, saying: "This is totally untrue. In line with the rules at the time the prime minister hosted a small number of family members outside that evening".<ref name="auto12" /> More than two people were forbidden to socialise indoors, while up to six were allowed outdoors.<ref name="BBC_60121572" /> Sue Gray, who ran an inquiry into events, was reported to have known about the event prior to the news breaking.<ref name="guardlive24" />
On 25 January, in an interview with [[Channel 4 News]], Northern Ireland Minister [[Conor Burns]] explained his understanding of the Prime Minister's role in the event. He said people came to the Cabinet room, where Johnson was working, and presented him with a cake. When pressed, Burns summarised it saying Johnson was, "in a sense, ambushed with a cake".<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.channel4.com/news/boris-johnson-was-ambushed-with-a-cake-says-conservative-mp|title=Boris Johnson was 'ambushed with a cake', says Conservative MP|last=Newman|first=Cathy|date=25 January 2022|work=[[Channel 4 News|4 News]]|access-date=26 January 2022|archive-date=25 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220125222541/https://www.channel4.com/news/boris-johnson-was-ambushed-with-a-cake-says-conservative-mp|url-status=live}}</ref> Burns' comments were ridiculed online in a series of [[Internet meme|memes]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2022/01/26/boris-johnson-lockdown-party-ambushed-by-cake/|title=Was Boris Johnson birthday partying in lockdown or 'ambushed with a cake'? Brits mock the latest defense.|last=Hassan|first=Jennifer|date=26 January 2022|newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]|access-date=26 January 2022|archive-date=26 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220126124151/https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2022/01/26/boris-johnson-lockdown-party-ambushed-by-cake/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.pinknews.co.uk/2022/01/26/nigella-lawson-conor-burns-boris-johnson-cake/|title=Nigella Lawson leads roasting of Tory MP who claimed Boris Johnson was 'ambushed by cake'|last=Milton|first=Josh|date=26 January 2022|work=[[PinkNews]]|access-date=26 January 2022|archive-date=26 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220126100928/https://www.pinknews.co.uk/2022/01/26/nigella-lawson-conor-burns-boris-johnson-cake/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.thenational.scot/news/19875227.nigella-lawson-roasts-conor-burns-ambushed-cake-claim/|title=Nigella Lawson roasts Conor Burns for 'ambushed with cake' claim|date=26 January 2022|work=[[The National (Scotland)|The National]]|access-date=27 January 2022|archive-date=27 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220127122316/https://www.thenational.scot/news/19875227.nigella-lawson-roasts-conor-burns-ambushed-cake-claim/|url-status=live}}</ref> Burns later told ''[[The Daily Telegraph]]''{{'}}s podcast that he had since been told "that there actually wasn't a cake".<ref name="guard220128">{{cite news |last1=Slawson |first1=Nicola |last2=Allegretti |first2=Aubrey |last3=Weaver |first3=Matthew |last4=Syal |first4=Rajeev |title=Downing Street parties: No 10 denies talking to Met police about Gray report and what could be published – as it happened |url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/live/2022/jan/28/boris-johnson-gray-report-partygate-downing-street-parties-met-police-covid-coronavirus-live-news?page=with:block-61f3e3128f086b55f08187b5#block-61f3e3128f086b55f08187b5 |access-date=28 January 2022 |work=The Guardian |date=28 January 2022 |archive-date=28 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220128172559/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/live/2022/jan/28/boris-johnson-gray-report-partygate-downing-street-parties-met-police-covid-coronavirus-live-news?page=with:block-61f3e3128f086b55f08187b5#block-61f3e3128f086b55f08187b5 |url-status=live }}</ref> ''The Times'' had published an article in June 2020 reporting the gathering on Johnson's birthday and that he "tucked into a Union Jack cake".<ref name="guard220128"/><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.thetimes.com/uk/politics/article/rishi-sunak-settles-in-as-downing-sts-captain-sensible-t7xr2689g|title=Rishi Sunak settles in as Downing St's Captain Sensible|author1=Steven Swinford|author2=Oliver Wright|work=The Times|access-date=3 May 2024|archive-date=24 June 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230624074359/https://www.thetimes.co.uk/article/rishi-sunak-settles-in-as-downing-sts-captain-sensible-t7xr2689g|url-status=live}}</ref>
Starmer again called on Johnson to resign. Conservative peer [[Baroness Warsi]] said Johnson should, "think long and hard about what is in the best interest of this country. (...) Is me staying in office allowing me to run this office in a way in which is making the country better, or am I a distraction?"<ref name="BBC_60121572" /> On 28 January, Starmer said that "the whole of government is paralysed because the police are now looking at what the PM was getting up to in Downing Street".<ref>{{Cite web |last=Eden |first=Tom |date=2022-01-28 |title=Keir Starmer: Government is 'paralysed by partygate and criminal investigation' |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/government-prime-minister-boris-johnson-sue-gray-downing-street-b2002823.html |access-date=2023-03-27 |website=The Independent |language=en |archive-date=4 May 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240504005212/https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/government-prime-minister-boris-johnson-sue-gray-downing-street-b2002823.html |url-status=live }}</ref> [[Alistair Carmichael]] (Liberal Democrat) and [[Ian Blackford]] (SNP) both said that the delay increasingly gave the appearance of an establishment "stitch-up".<ref>{{Cite web |last=Payne |first=Adam |date=2022-01-28 |title=Met Police Move To Water Down Gray Report Accused Of Looking Like A Stitch Up |url=https://www.politicshome.com/news/article/met-police-sue-gray-stitch-up-opposition |access-date=2023-03-27 |website=Politics Home |language=en |archive-date=28 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220128145133/https://www.politicshome.com/news/article/met-police-sue-gray-stitch-up-opposition |url-status=live }}</ref>
Late on 29 January, ''The Times'' reported that Gray had discovered that friends of [[Carrie Symonds]]' knew the [[PIN code]] to access her flat with Boris Johnson in 11 Downing Street. Cummings said that, in early 2020, he discovered highly confidential Government documents were left lying around in the flat, leading to him and [[Martin Reynolds (civil servant)|Martin Reynolds]] instituting tighter controls on what papers left Johnson's office.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://uk.sports.yahoo.com/news/fears-over-top-secret-papers-202457708.html |title=Fears over top-secret papers left strewn by Boris Johnson revealed |work=The National |date=29 January 2022 |via=Yahoo!sport |access-date=29 January 2022 |archive-date=29 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220129230640/https://uk.sports.yahoo.com/news/fears-over-top-secret-papers-202457708.html |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |last=Shipman |first=Tim |title=Saving Boris Johnson's bacon: is the PM wriggling free of Partygate? |work=The Times |url=https://www.thetimes.com/uk/politics/article/is-boris-johnson-wriggling-free-of-partygate-hn9rlbkrw |url-status=live |url-access=subscription |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220129182750/https://www.thetimes.co.uk/article/is-boris-johnson-wriggling-free-of-partygate-hn9rlbkrw |archive-date=29 January 2022 |via=}}</ref>[[File:Downing Street garden balcony (7176412585).jpg|thumb|Gray commented on the use of the [[Garden of 10 and 11 Downing Street]] in her update.]]Unable to publish her full report because of the ongoing police investigation, Gray published an update on her investigation on 31 January 2022. This covered a number of events that had not previously been reported. There was an accompanying debate in the Commons. Starmer said: "The prime minister repeatedly assured the House that the guidance was followed and the rules were followed. But we now know that 12 cases have breached the threshold for criminal investigation (...) including the party on May 20, 2020, which we know the prime minister attended, and the party on November 13, 2020 in the prime minister's flat. There can be no doubt that the prime minister himself is now subject to criminal investigation. (...) it is already clear what the report disclosed is the most damning conclusion possible".<ref name=":25">{{Cite news |last1=Otte |first1=Jedidajah |last2=Sparrow |first2=Andrew |date=2022-01-31 |title=Boris Johnson apologises in wake of Sue Gray report as Met police given 300 photos linked to No 10 parties – as it happened |language=en-GB |work=the Guardian |url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/live/2022/jan/31/uk-politics-live-omicron-nhs-workers-coronavirus-vaccines-no-10-sue-gray-report |access-date=2023-03-27 |issn=0261-3077 |archive-date=31 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220131154313/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/live/2022/jan/31/uk-politics-live-omicron-nhs-workers-coronavirus-vaccines-no-10-sue-gray-report |url-status=live }}</ref>
{{Anchor|Savilegate}} In the Commons debate on the topic, Johnson said Starmer "spent most of his time [as Director of Public Prosecutions] prosecuting journalists [a reference to [[Julian Assange]]'s prosecution] and failing to prosecute [[Jimmy Savile]]". [[Full Fact]] fact checkers said: "Starmer was head of the CPS when the decision was made not to prosecute Savile but he was not the reviewing lawyer for the case". The BBC "found no evidence that Sir Keir was involved at any point in the decision not to charge Savile". [[Speaker of the House of Commons (United Kingdom)|Speaker of the House of Commons]] [[Lindsay Hoyle]] also criticised Johnson's accusation.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Allen-Kinross |first=Pippa |date=26 June 2020 |title=Keir Starmer led the CPS when it did not charge Jimmy Savile, but he wasn't the reviewing lawyer |url=https://fullfact.org/online/keir-starmer-prosecute-jimmy-savile/ |website=Full Fact |access-date=3 February 2022 |archive-date=3 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220203204517/https://fullfact.org/online/keir-starmer-prosecute-jimmy-savile/ |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/60213975|title=No evidence for Boris Johnson's claim about Keir Starmer and Jimmy Savile|work=BBC News|date=3 February 2022|access-date=3 February 2022|archive-date=8 February 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220208163011/https://www.bbc.com/news/60213975|url-status=live}}</ref> Three days later, Johnson stated that he was "not talking about the leader of the opposition's personal record when he was DPP. I was making a point about his responsibility for the organisation as a whole". [[Munira Mirza]], Director of the [[Number 10 Policy Unit]], resigned on 3 February, saying Johnson should have apologised. On the same day, [[Jack Doyle (journalist)|Jack Doyle]] quit as Johnson's communications director.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-60250036|title=Boris Johnson's policy chief Munira Mirza resigns over PM's Savile remarks|work=BBC News|date=3 February 2022|access-date=3 February 2022|archive-date=3 February 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220203160344/https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-60250036|url-status=live}}</ref> Doyle talked about the difficulty of his job in recent weeks, but also said that he had always intended to only work in government for two years. Starmer later needed police protection, following a mob threatening violence outside parliament. Starmer and others blamed Johnson for inciting the disturbance. Starmer experienced online death threats, he said: "It's very important for me to say that what the prime minister said was wrong, it was very wrong. He knew exactly what he was doing".<ref>{{Cite news |date=2022-02-14 |title=Sir Keir Starmer confirms he had death threats after PM's Jimmy Savile claim |language=en-GB |work=BBC News |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-60373912 |access-date=2023-03-27 |archive-date=27 March 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230327062704/https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-60373912 |url-status=live }}</ref>
Cummings said there were photos of the alleged 13 November party in the Johnsons' flat.<ref name=allwecan />
On 4 February, 10 Downing Street announced the resignations of both [[Dan Rosenfield]], Johnson's chief of staff, and Martin Reynolds, his principal private secretary. Sky News described this as "an apparent mass exodus from Downing Street amid the fallout from the partygate scandal" (by 22 April they described it as "part of a purge of the prime minister's senior team in the wake of the partygate scandal"). Rosenfield and Reynolds were to continue in their roles until their successors were appointed.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Heffer |first=Greg |date=4 February 2022 |title=Four of Boris Johnson's senior aides quit Number 10 in mass exodus amid partygate fallout |url=https://news.sky.com/story/two-more-senior-officials-quit-number-10-amid-partygate-fallout-12532195 |work=Sky News |access-date=3 February 2022 |archive-date=3 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220203205024/https://news.sky.com/story/two-more-senior-officials-quit-number-10-amid-partygate-fallout-12532195 |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |last=Morris |first=Sophie |date=22 April 2022 |title=Downing Street parties: At least one No 10 official fined for lockdown-busting event, Sky News understands |work=Sky News |url=https://news.sky.com/story/downing-street-parties-at-least-one-no-10-official-fined-for-lockdown-busting-event-sky-news-understands-12596144 |access-date=22 April 2022 resigned the following day.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Duggan |first=Joe |date=4 February 2022 |title=Fifth member of No 10 team quits as Boris Johnson faces fresh Downing Street turmoil |url=https://inews.co.uk/news/downing-street-resignations-no-10-team-resigns-boris-johnson-policy-unit-1442826 |website=i |access-date=4 February 2022 |archive-date=4 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220204094447/https://inews.co.uk/news/downing-street-resignations-no-10-team-resigns-boris-johnson-policy-unit-1442826 |url-status=live }}</ref> BBC News reported that [[Minister of State for Energy Security and Net Zero|energy minister]], [[Greg Hands]] said the resignations came after Johnson "made it clear there would be a shake-up" in Downing Street organisation, following criticism of it in the Gray investigation update.<ref name=":7">{{Cite news |date=2022-02-03 |title=Boris Johnson rocked by wave of No 10 resignations |language=en-GB |work=BBC News |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-60253231 |access-date=2023-03-27 |archive-date=23 March 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230323025517/https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-60253231 |url-status=live }}</ref> Mirza considered Johnson's comment "scurrilous".<ref>{{Cite news |last=Stewart |first=Heather |date=2022-02-03 |title=Four Johnson aides quit in fallout from Downing Street parties |language=en-GB |work=The Guardian |url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/feb/03/boris-johnsons-policy-chief-quits-over-pms-scurrilous-savile-remark |access-date=2023-03-27 |issn=0261-3077 |archive-date=22 April 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230422055919/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/feb/03/boris-johnsons-policy-chief-quits-over-pms-scurrilous-savile-remark |url-status=live }}</ref> The ''Daily Mirror'' reported sources saying the police had a photograph of Johnson with a can of [[Estrella Damm|Estrella beer]] standing next to Rishi Sunak at the 19 June 2020 Cabinet Room birthday event. It is reported to be one of the 300 images given by Gray to the Metropolitan Police, and to have been taken by the official No 10 photographer, [[Andrew Parsons (photographer)|Andrew Parsons]].<ref>{{Cite web|url = https://www.mirror.co.uk/news/politics/bombshell-picture-shows-boris-johnson-26144515|title = Bombshell pic shows Boris Johnson holding beer at lockdown birthday party|website = [[Daily Mirror]]|date = 4 February 2022|access-date = 4 February 2022|archive-date = 4 February 2022|archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20220204210017/https://www.mirror.co.uk/news/politics/bombshell-picture-shows-boris-johnson-26144515|url-status = live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |last=Badshah |first=Nadeem |date=4 February 2022 |title=Police have photo of Boris Johnson with beer at lockdown party, say reports |work=[[The Guardian]] |url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/feb/04/police-have-photo-of-boris-johnson-with-beer-at-lockdown-party-say-reports |access-date=5 February 2022 |archive-date=5 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220205202330/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/feb/04/police-have-photo-of-boris-johnson-with-beer-at-lockdown-party-say-reports |url-status=live }}</ref>
During Prime Minister's Questions on 9 February, the ''Daily Mirror'' released another photograph from the 15 December 2020 Christmas quiz, showing Johnson and three other people, one wearing tinsel, one a Santa hat and "what appears to be champagne and a half-eaten packet of crisps".<ref>{{Cite news |last=Sparrow |first=Andrew |date=9 February 2022 |title=Boris Johnson says he intends to scrap Covid isolation rules as new 'party' photo emerges – live |work=The Guardian |url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/live/2022/feb/09/boris-johnson-pmqs-keir-starmer-uk-politics-live-latest |via= |access-date=3 May 2024 |archive-date=21 June 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230621085239/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/live/2022/feb/09/boris-johnson-pmqs-keir-starmer-uk-politics-live-latest |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/news/politics/new-partygate-image-shows-boris-26181071|title=New bombshell pic shows Boris Johnson and open bottle of bubbly at No 10 quiz|first=Pippa|last=Crerar|date=9 February 2022|work=Daily Mirror|access-date=3 May 2024|archive-date=11 February 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240211082518/https://www.mirror.co.uk/news/politics/new-partygate-image-shows-boris-26181071|url-status=live}}</ref>
==== After the conclusion of the police investigation ==== Three anonymous Downing Street insiders spoke to the BBC ''[[Panorama (British TV programme)|Panorama]]'' programme about the conditions, culture and morale inside government offices during the COVID-19 lockdown. The insiders described how, as Cain's leaving do developed, "there were about 30 people, if not more, in a room. Everyone was stood shoulder to shoulder, some people on each other's laps... one or two people". They characterised the event on the day before Prince Philip's funeral as a "lively event... a general party with people dancing around." They added that security guards told them to leave the building and go into the garden when it became too loud. They said events were routine and that some staff were worried about the potential consequences, but that it seemed difficult to raise concerns. One said that, for staff who were working long hours, it was a lifeline. They all felt that the culture developed and it seemed the prime minister "wanted to be liked" and for staff to be able to "let their hair down."<ref name=":12">{{Cite web |last=Kuenssberg |first=Laura |date=24 May 2022 |title=Partygate: Insiders tell of packed No 10 lockdown parties |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-politics-61566410 |website=BBC News |access-date=24 May 2022 |archive-date=24 May 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220524142305/https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-politics-61566410 |url-status=live }}</ref> The BBC's Laura Kuenssberg reported on 24 May 2022 that the prime minister's official spokesman had said that Johnson took the revelations about what happened during lockdown "very seriously". The spokesman added that [[Sue Gray (political adviser)|Sue Gray]]'s interim report "raised some of these challenges" and that as a result, "wholesale changes" had been made to the way No 10 operated with "further changes to come."<ref name=":12" />
One senior figure said: "No 10 staff were 'essential workers' because the office is the UK's strategic headquarters. It is extraordinary that the government risked the strategic HQ's capability by risking a single [Covid] outbreak closing the building completely, leaving the UK unguided."<ref>{{Cite news |last=Savage |first=Michael |date=2022-05-29 |title=Don't make Partygate official Martin Reynolds our man in Riyadh, urge Tories |language=en-GB |work=The Observer |url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/may/29/partygate-official-martin-reynolds-riyadh |access-date=2023-03-27 |issn=0029-7712 |archive-date=21 June 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230621085748/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/may/29/partygate-official-martin-reynolds-riyadh |url-status=live }}</ref>
==== After the publication of Gray's report ==== When presenting the report to the [[House of Commons of the United Kingdom|House of Commons]] on the afternoon of 25 May 2022, Johnson said he was humbled and took full responsibility, and renewed his apology for the 19 June 2020 Cabinet Office event which led to him receiving a [[fixed penalty notice]] (FPN). He said that at events he had briefly attended to thank staff, he had not known about subsequent rule breaking now revealed. Improvements had been made to organisation and senior management. Starmer said the government had set a low standard for conduct, and stated his own pledge to resign if a FPN was given to him for the [[Beergate|Durham event]]. Johnson was asked by Conservative [[Robert Buckland]] if he had "deliberately lied" when telling the Commons that rules had been followed, Johnson said no, he had believed he was attending work events and, except for the Cabinet Office event, "that has been vindicated by the investigation".<ref name="Sky News 25 May">{{cite web | title=Sue Gray partygate report: 'Humbled' Boris Johnson 'surprised and disappointed' at revelations | website=Sky News | date=25 May 2022 | url=https://news.sky.com/story/boris-johnson-says-he-was-surprised-and-disappointed-at-partygate-revelations-in-sue-gray-report-12621061 | access-date=7 June 2022 | archive-date=7 June 2022 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220607195527/https://news.sky.com/story/boris-johnson-says-he-was-surprised-and-disappointed-at-partygate-revelations-in-sue-gray-report-12621061 | url-status=live }}</ref><ref name="Sir Beer Korma 25 May 22">{{cite web | last=Wheeler | first=Richard | title=PM makes 'Sir Beer Korma' jibe shortly after insisting Gray report 'humbled' him | website=Evening Standard | date=25 May 2022 | url=https://www.standard.co.uk/news/uk/keir-starmer-boris-johnson-sue-gray-mps-commons-b1002324.html | access-date=30 May 2022 | archive-date=31 May 2022 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220531213418/https://www.standard.co.uk/news/uk/keir-starmer-boris-johnson-sue-gray-mps-commons-b1002324.html | url-status=live }}</ref><ref name="Sue Gray Report Hansard">{{cite web|date=25 May 2022|title=Sue Gray Report: Volume 715: debated on Wednesday 25 May 2022|url=https://hansard.parliament.uk/Commons/2022-05-25/debates/E888D0F8-37F7-48A5-8598-4449887A0935/details|access-date=31 May 2022|website=UK Parliament: Hansard|archive-date=28 June 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220628023846/https://hansard.parliament.uk/Commons/2022-05-25/debates/E888D0F8-37F7-48A5-8598-4449887A0935/details|url-status=live}}</ref>
It is alleged that there was a gathering in the Johnsons' flat on 19 June 2020, the Prime Minister's birthday. This was not mentioned in Gray's report. The allegation is that a Downing Street aide received text messages from [[Carrie Johnson]] (then Carrie Symonds) that said she was in the flat with two friends, with Boris Johnson later joining them. A spokesperson for Carrie Johnson said that Gray had been made aware of these texts, but an aide with copies of the texts said, according to ''The Sunday Times'', that they wrote to Case about them, offering to share them with Gray's team, but was ignored. Labour called for a further investigation.<ref>{{cite news |last=Allegretti |first=Aubrey |date=29 May 2022 |title=Partygate: Labour urges inquiry into alleged second event at No 10 hosted by Carrie Johnson |work=The Guardian |url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/may/29/partygate-sue-gray-knew-carrie-johnson-texts-birthday-party-whitehall |access-date=3 May 2024 |archive-date=11 April 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240411092356/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/may/29/partygate-sue-gray-knew-carrie-johnson-texts-birthday-party-whitehall |url-status=live }}</ref>
On 7 June 2022, [[Patrick Vallance]], the Government's chief scientific adviser, in a reply to a question about whether Partygate had tarnished his feelings about the time during the pandemic that he spent in government said, "it was really important at all stages that everyone stuck to the rules [...] It only works when people stick to them and it's very disappointing that that wasn't the case".<ref>{{cite news |last=Lee |first=Dulcie |date=7 June 2022 |title=Partygate: Sir Patrick Vallance says it is 'disappointing' No 10 broke rules |work=BBC News |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-61723492 |access-date=8 June 2022 |archive-date=8 June 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220608070930/https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-61723492 |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |last=Sparrow |first=Andrew |date=7 June 2022 |title=Boris Johnson's no-confidence vote: PM tells cabinet to 'draw a line' under Partygate after narrowly surviving bruising ballot – as it happened |work=The Guardian |url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/live/2022/jun/07/boris-johnson-no-confidence-vote-conservatives-rebels-uk-politics-latest?filterKeyEvents=false&page=with:block-629f49e88f0813609ca69259 |access-date=8 June 2022 |archive-date=8 June 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220608214620/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/live/2022/jun/07/boris-johnson-no-confidence-vote-conservatives-rebels-uk-politics-latest?filterKeyEvents=false&page=with:block-629f49e88f0813609ca69259 |url-status=live }}</ref>
[[The Guardian]] has carried reports that [[cocaine]] residue was found after two of the events at 10 Downing Street.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Isaac |first=Anna |date=16 December 2022 |title=Traces of suspected cocaine found after parties in Liz Truss's grace-and-favour house, say staff |work=The Guardian |url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/dec/16/traces-of-suspected-cocaine-found-after-parties-in-liz-truss-grace-and-favour-house-say-staff |access-date=19 December 2022 |archive-date=19 December 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221219012352/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/dec/16/traces-of-suspected-cocaine-found-after-parties-in-liz-truss-grace-and-favour-house-say-staff |url-status=live }}</ref>
===2023=== In January 2023, ITV News reported allegations from an anonymous source who worked in Downing Street that Johnson had joked to staff at Lee Cain's leaving event (13 November 2020) that "this is the most unsocially distanced party in the UK right now".<ref name=podcast1 /> A spokesperson for Johnson did not deny that he made the comment.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Crerar |first1=Pippa |title=Boris Johnson reportedly joked about being at 'UK's most unsocially distanced party' during lockdown |url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2023/jan/11/boris-johnson-reportedly-joked-he-was-at-most-unsocially-distanced-party-in-uk |access-date=11 January 2023 |work=The Guardian |date=11 January 2023 |language=en |archive-date=11 January 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230111115204/https://www.theguardian.com/world/2023/jan/11/boris-johnson-reportedly-joked-he-was-at-most-unsocially-distanced-party-in-uk |url-status=live }}</ref>
ITV News also reported claims by Downing Street staff that individuals had shared their stories before filling out the Metropolitan Police questionnaires, that some staff shredded documents or did not hand over photographs of events,<ref name=podcast4 /> and that only half of the events were ever investigated by Sue Gray or the police.<ref name=itv23jan>{{Cite web|url=https://www.itv.com/news/2023-01-10/exclusive-boris-johnson-joked-about-most-unsocially-distanced-party-in-the-uk|title=Boris Johnson joked about 'most unsocially distanced party in the UK'|date=12 January 2023|website=ITV News|access-date=11 January 2023|archive-date=11 January 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230111124921/https://www.itv.com/news/2023-01-10/exclusive-boris-johnson-joked-about-most-unsocially-distanced-party-in-the-uk|url-status=live}}</ref> Staff were also alleged to have had sex at the event on the evening prior to Prince Philip's funeral.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Grylls |first1=George |last2=Zeffman |first2=Henry |title=Boris Johnson aides 'had sex' at No 10 lockdown party |url=https://www.thetimes.com/uk/politics/article/boris-johnson-partygate-joked-about-lack-of-social-distancing-at-downing-street-lockdown-event-qh5fcw9pz |access-date=12 January 2023 |date=12 January 2023 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20230112104008/https://www.thetimes.co.uk/article/boris-johnson-partygate-joked-about-lack-of-social-distancing-at-downing-street-lockdown-event-qh5fcw9pz |archive-date=12 January 2023 |url-status=live |language=en}}</ref>
ITV News also reported allegations that wine time Fridays, end of the week drinks parties held by the Number 10 press office, continued through lockdown with Johnson often attending, without wearing a face mask. Staff say that when they raised concerns with senior civil servants about breaches of COVID-19 regulations, their concerns were dismissed.<ref name=podcast1 /> Staff also alleged that there were additional parties that have never been reported.<ref name=podcast6>{{cite podcast |title=Partygate: The Inside Story - new revelations and our whistleblowers in their own words: 6. Getting Away With It? |website=ITV News |date=12 January 2023 |url=https://www.itv.com/news/2023-01-11/partygate-the-inside-story-reveals-more-on-downing-streets-notorious-parties |access-date=3 May 2024 |archive-date=6 April 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240406105915/https://www.itv.com/news/2023-01-11/partygate-the-inside-story-reveals-more-on-downing-streets-notorious-parties |url-status=live }}</ref>
Unmesh Desai, the deputy chair of the London Assembly's police and crime committee, wrote to the Metropolitan Police commissioner, Mark Rowley, asking them to re-open their investigation. Desai said that new evidence directly contradicted Johnson's claim he did not know about rulebreaking at 10 Downing Street, and said: "I have raised the apparent inconsistency in how the MPS [Metropolitan Police Service] have approached the investigation with your predecessors – particularly regarding why the former prime minister was issued with only one fixed penalty notice for his birthday party, but not for the other events, including the leaving drinks where there is photographic evidence of him holding a glass of champagne and making a toast."<ref>{{Cite news |last=Townsend |first=Mark |date=2023-02-11 |title=Met urged to reopen Partygate inquiry as fresh allegations emerge |language=en-GB |work=The Observer |url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2023/feb/11/partygate-fresh-allegations-met-urged-to-reopen-inquiry |access-date=2023-03-27 |issn=0029-7712 |archive-date=11 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230211135154/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2023/feb/11/partygate-fresh-allegations-met-urged-to-reopen-inquiry |url-status=live }}</ref>
====COVID-19 Inquiry evidence==== In May 2023, in preparation of material for the [[COVID-19 Inquiry]], the [[Cabinet Office]] found that Johnson's diary showed him hosting visits by friends and family to [[Chequers]] between June 2020 and May 2021, as well as further events at Downing Street. They referred the matter to the police, who they contacted on 16 May 2023, as possible breaches of COVID-19 restrictions. A report was received by [[Thames Valley Police]] (for Chequers) on 18 May and by the Metropolitan Police (for Downing Street) on 19 May.<ref name=undisclosed /> A spokesperson for Johnson said, "Lawyers have examined the events in question and advised that they were lawful."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://news.sky.com/story/boris-johnson-referred-to-police-over-fresh-claims-of-covid-lockdown-rule-breaking-12887865|title=Boris Johnson blasts 'bizarre and unacceptable' new claims he broke COVID lockdown rules|website=Sky News|access-date=13 June 2023|archive-date=21 June 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230621084326/https://news.sky.com/story/boris-johnson-referred-to-police-over-fresh-claims-of-covid-lockdown-rule-breaking-12887865|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2023/may/23/boris-johnson-referred-to-police-over-allegedly-hosting-friends-at-chequers-in-lockdown|title=Boris Johnson referred to police over allegedly hosting friends at Chequers in lockdown|first1=Pippa|last1=Crerar|first2=Aubrey|last2=Allegretti|date=23 May 2023|access-date=13 June 2023|newspaper=The Guardian|archive-date=15 June 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230615000823/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2023/may/23/boris-johnson-referred-to-police-over-allegedly-hosting-friends-at-chequers-in-lockdown|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |author1=[[Henry Zeffman]] |author2=Steven Swinford |author3=Fiona Hamilton |date=13 June 2023 |title=Boris Johnson referred to police over new lockdown rulebreaking claims |url=https://www.thetimes.com/uk/politics/article/boris-johnson-referred-police-broke-more-lockdown-rules-chequers-family-friends-0h0msqnld |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230610103824/https://www.thetimes.co.uk/article/boris-johnson-referred-police-broke-more-lockdown-rules-chequers-family-friends-0h0msqnld |archive-date=10 June 2023 |access-date=13 June 2023 |via=www.thetimes.co.uk}}</ref> ''The Guardian'' reported that there were about 12 events, including ones not previously investigated by the police or covered by [[Sue Gray (civil servant)|Sue Gray]]'s report.<ref name=undisclosed>{{Cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2023/may/24/undisclosed-covid-era-johnson-events-occurred-at-both-chequers-and-downing-street|title=Undisclosed Covid-era Johnson events occurred at both Chequers and Downing Street|first=Aubrey|last=Allegretti|date=24 May 2023|access-date=13 June 2023|newspaper=The Guardian|archive-date=14 June 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230614040446/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2023/may/24/undisclosed-covid-era-johnson-events-occurred-at-both-chequers-and-downing-street|url-status=live}}</ref>
Subsequent reports in ''The Sunday Times'' alleged visits to Chequers by [[Richard Sharp (banker)|Richard Sharp]] (then BBC Chair), Sam Blyth (Johnson's cousin) and Conservative peer [[David Brownlow, Baron Brownlow of Shurlock Row|David Brownlow]], as well as an overnight stay by two friends of Carrie Johnson in May 2021.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/politics/boris-johnson-partygate-chequers-richard-sharp-b2347629.html|title='Tedious' Boris on the back foot as more Chequers diary details revealed|date=29 May 2023|website=The Independent|access-date=13 June 2023|archive-date=5 June 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230605175945/https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/politics/boris-johnson-partygate-chequers-richard-sharp-b2347629.html|url-status=live}}</ref> [[Rachel Johnson]], Boris's sister, has talked of visiting Chequers during this period saying, "as far as I am aware, all the rules were followed whenever I went to Chequers".<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2023/may/25/chillaxing-at-chequers-how-boris-johnson-used-the-pms-country-house|title=Chillaxing at Chequers: how Boris Johnson used the PM's country house|first=Heather|last=Stewart|date=25 May 2023|access-date=13 June 2023|newspaper=The Guardian|archive-date=3 June 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230603045116/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2023/may/25/chillaxing-at-chequers-how-boris-johnson-used-the-pms-country-house|url-status=live}}</ref>
The decision to refer the matter to the police was made by civil servants and the current Prime Minister, [[Rishi Sunak]], and his government were not involved.<ref name=undisclosed />
In June, the Privileges Committee Inquiry revealed that there were 16 events referred to police.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/news/politics/16-more-lockdown-gatherings-no10-30246277|title=16 more gatherings at No10 and Chequers uncovered in Boris Johnson's diaries|first=John|last=Stevens|date=15 June 2023|website=mirror|access-date=3 May 2024|archive-date=6 January 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240106201923/https://www.mirror.co.uk/news/politics/16-more-lockdown-gatherings-no10-30246277|url-status=live}}</ref>
On 4 July 2023, the Metropolitan and Thames Valley Police jointly announced that they would not launch an investigation into events at Chequers and 10 Downing Street referred to them by Cabinet Office officials. A statement said that following "further clarification" on the diary entries, they had decided that the events did "not meet the retrospective criteria for opening an investigation".<ref name=policereopen />
====June–October 2023==== On 9 June 2023, [[2022 Prime Minister's Resignation Honours (Boris Johnson)|Johnson's resignation honours list]] was published and gave honours for various people involved in Partygate events, including [[Martin Reynolds (civil servant)|Martin Reynolds]]. [[Shaun Bailey, Baron Bailey of Paddington|Shaun Bailey]] was given a peerage, while Ben Mallet, the campaign director for the party's 2021 London Mayoral election, was given an [[OBE]]. Both had been pictured at the 14 December 2020 gathering at Conservative Campaign Headquarters. [[Shelley Williams-Walker]], who was head of operations for No 10 at the time, was appointed [[Dame Commander of the Order of the British Empire]] (DBE). She was alleged to have been in charge of music at the 15 April 2021 party.<ref name="auto14">{{Cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/boris-johnson-reynolds-downing-street-angela-rayner-labour-b2354868.html|title=Senior aides implicated in partygate nominated for honours by Boris Johnson|date=10 June 2023|website=The Independent|access-date=13 June 2023|archive-date=10 June 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230610002509/https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/boris-johnson-reynolds-downing-street-angela-rayner-labour-b2354868.html|url-status=live}}</ref> There was also an honour for [[Jack Doyle (journalist)|Jack Doyle]], Johnson's former director of communications when the Partygate story first broke and who had been present at an 18 December 2020 event;<ref name="auto14" /> a [[peerage]] was given to chief of staff [[Dan Rosenfield]], who was in office during the period of Partygate events; and a CBE was given to Rosie Bate-Williams, a press adviser who handled some of Johnson's denials.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-65904441|title=PMQs: Keir Starmer attacks Rishi Sunak over Boris Johnson honours list|work=BBC News|date=14 June 2023|access-date=18 June 2023|archive-date=18 June 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230618094636/https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-65904441|url-status=live}}</ref>
Later the same day, Johnson resigned as an MP, in response to the [[Commons Privileges Committee investigation into Boris Johnson|Commons Privilege Committee inquiry]] into him.<ref name="auto15">{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/uk-politics-65860705|title=Boris Johnson: Tories to face third by-election as ally of former PM resigns|date=10 June 2023|website=BBC News|access-date=13 June 2023|archive-date=13 June 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230613072328/https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/uk-politics-65860705|url-status=live}}</ref> The Commons Privilege Committee inquiry reported on 15 June 2023.<ref name="BBC_65876914" /> In additional material, the Committee released a written report from an anonymous No. 10 official, received on 7 February 2023.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Zeffman|first1=Henry|title=10 key points from the privileges committee report on Boris Johnson |url=https://www.thetimes.com/uk/politics/article/boris-johnson-report-privileges-committee-key-points-8jbsztddr |access-date=15 June 2023 |date=15 June 2023 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20230615145634/https://www.thetimes.co.uk/article/boris-johnson-report-privileges-committee-key-points-8jbsztddr |archive-date=15 June 2023 |url-status=live|language=en}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=Stone |first1=Jon |title=Revealed: Downing Street staff's 'pantomime' to hide No.10 lockdown breaches |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/politics/downing-street-lockdown-partygate-pantomime-boris-johnson-b2358179.html |access-date=15 June 2023 |work=The Independent |date=15 June 2023 |language=en}}</ref>
On 17 June, the ''Daily Mirror'' released a short video clip, from the 14 December 2020 Christmas party at Conservative Campaign Headquarters, showing people ignoring social distancing, including two people dancing together closely. An attendee could be heard saying it was OK to film "as long as we don't stream that we're, like, bending the rules".<ref name="Gove"/><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://news.sky.com/story/partygate-conservative-party-staff-filmed-drinking-and-dancing-during-covid-lockdown-12904492|title=Partygate: Conservative Party staff filmed drinking and dancing during COVID lockdown|website=Sky News|access-date=18 June 2023|archive-date=18 June 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230618062809/https://news.sky.com/story/partygate-conservative-party-staff-filmed-drinking-and-dancing-during-covid-lockdown-12904492|url-status=live}}</ref> [[Shaun Bailey, Baron Bailey of Paddington|Shaun Bailey]] had been in attendance, but had left before the video was taken. The video showed Ben Mallet.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/news/politics/first-ever-partygate-video-revealed-30259486|title=Tories drink, dance and laugh at Covid rules in first ever Partygate video|first1=John|last1=Stevens|first2=Mikey|last2=Smith|date=17 June 2023|website=mirror|access-date=18 June 2023|archive-date=18 June 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230618062820/https://www.mirror.co.uk/news/politics/first-ever-partygate-video-revealed-30259486|url-status=live}}</ref> On 17 June, Secretary of State for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities [[Michael Gove]] apologised for the video, saying it was "indefensible".<ref name="Gove">{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-65941549|title=Michael Gove says sorry over Tory lockdown party video|work=BBC News|date=17 June 2023|access-date=18 June 2023|archive-date=18 June 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230618094635/https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-65941549|url-status=live}}</ref> Bailey apologised "unreservedly" over the video on 19 June.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/uk-politics-65935357 | title=Boris Johnson Partygate report: MPS vote to accept findings | work=BBC News | date=19 June 2023 | access-date=19 June 2023 | archive-date=19 June 2023 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230619173409/https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/live/uk-politics-65935357 | url-status=live }}</ref>
On 18 June, the BBC reported the invitation, sent out on behalf of Mallet, for the Christmas party, which described it with the words "Jingle and Mingle".<ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-politics-65952298 | title=Tory aides invited to 'Jingle and Mingle' Covid party | work=BBC News | date=19 June 2023 | access-date=19 June 2023 | archive-date=19 June 2023 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230619192732/https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-politics-65952298 | url-status=live }}</ref>
The Conservative MP [[Tobias Ellwood]] said Bailey should consider declining his peerage.<ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-politics-65959578 | title=Jingle & Mingle party: Shaun Bailey should consider turning down peerage, says Tory MP | work=BBC News | date=20 June 2023 | access-date=20 June 2023 | archive-date=20 June 2023 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230620105400/https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-politics-65959578 | url-status=live }}</ref>
On 19 June, the Metropolitan Police said they were considering the video as it was not seen as part of their prior investigation.<ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2023/jun/19/partygate-met-police-assessing-footage-not-previously-provided | title=Partygate: Met chief hints at new inquiry after 'jingle and mingle' video | newspaper=The Guardian | date=19 June 2023 | last1=Dodd | first1=Vikram | last2=Police | first2=Vikram Dodd | access-date=19 June 2023 | archive-date=19 June 2023 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230619190114/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2023/jun/19/partygate-met-police-assessing-footage-not-previously-provided | url-status=live }}</ref> In July, they confirmed that they had re-opened their investigation into the event. They also said they were investigating an event in Parliament on 8 December 2020.<ref name=policereopen>{{cite news | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-politics-66103922 | title=Partygate: Police reopen investigation into Tory 'jingle and mingle' event | work=BBC News | date=4 July 2023 | access-date=7 July 2023 | archive-date=5 July 2023 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230705102213/https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-politics-66103922 | url-status=live }}</ref>
24 additional Fixed Penalty Notices for the event were recommended by the police in October 2023.<ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-politics-67106804 | title=Fines to be issued for Covid Christmas party at Tory HQ | work=BBC News | date=13 October 2023 | access-date=14 October 2023 | archive-date=14 October 2023 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231014073024/https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-politics-67106804 | url-status=live }}</ref>
===2025=== The leaking in September 2025 of a set of records from Johnson's private office suggested further lockdown breaches had occurred, including a meeting with [[David Brownlow]] on 6 November 2020, and Boris and Carrie Johnson hosting three guests on 20 June 2020.<ref>https://www.theguardian.com/uk-news/2025/sep/08/boris-johnson-had-dinner-in-lockdown-with-peer-funding-flat-refit-files-suggest</ref>
==Events== {{for|the dates and details of the varying restrictions|COVID-19 lockdown in the United Kingdom}}
In addition to the specific dated events listed below, "wine time Fridays" starting at 4pm were a regular occurrence at Downing Street throughout the period.<ref name="auto10" /><ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/politics/2022/05/24/boris-johnson-latest-news-sue-gray-report-release-partygate/ | title='Wine-Time Friday' at No 10 saw drinking start at 4pm, insiders reveal | work=The Telegraph | date=24 May 2022 | last1=Maidment | first1=Jack | last2=Penna | first2=Dominic | access-date=3 May 2024 | archive-date=21 June 2023 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230621141417/https://www.telegraph.co.uk/politics/2022/05/24/boris-johnson-latest-news-sue-gray-report-release-partygate/ | url-status=live }}</ref> [[Dominic Cummings]] has claimed there may have also been parties at [[Chequers]].<ref name="moore" /> The Department for Work and Pensions confirmed staff drank alcohol and ate [[takeaway food]] together "on a number of occasions" while working late during the period of COVID-19 restrictions.<ref name="auto5" />
On 22 January 2022, ''[[The Sunday Times]]'' reported that Gray was also investigating [[Carrie Johnson]] (then Carrie Symonds) hosting two friends (who worked at the time for [[Michael Gove]] at 70 Whitehall, part of the Downing Street complex) in the Downing Street flat multiple times during lockdown. It was stated that the visits were for "work reasons", but a Whitehall source said that no officials were present.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Shipman |first1=Tim |date=22 January 2022 |title=Will Boris Johnson run out of road? |url=https://www.thetimes.com/uk/politics/article/will-boris-johnson-run-out-of-road-7wlssszbj |work=The Times |access-date=26 January 2022 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20220122210349/https://www.thetimes.co.uk/article/will-boris-johnson-run-out-of-road-7wlssszbj |archive-date=22 January 2022 |url-status=live }}</ref> On 25 January 2022, the ''New Statesman'' published an analysis stating that "both Boris and Carrie Johnson do not believe they have done anything wrong, as they consider gatherings among those who worked at Downing Street during the pandemic to have been part of a 'household bubble'".<ref>{{Cite web |last=Lambert |first=Harry |date=2022-01-25 |title=Boris Johnson is entering his moment of greatest peril |url=https://www.newstatesman.com/politics/conservatives/2022/01/boris-johnson-is-entering-his-moment-of-greatest-peril |access-date=2023-03-27 |website=New Statesman |language=en-US |archive-date=25 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220125214853/https://www.newstatesman.com/politics/conservatives/2022/01/boris-johnson-is-entering-his-moment-of-greatest-peril |url-status=live }}</ref>
Gray looked into 16 events and the police at 12 of them, including 6 that Johnson reportedly attended.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Allegretti |first=Aubrey |date=2022-02-19 |title=Boris Johnson hands in police questionnaire on 'partygate' |language=en-GB |work=The Guardian |url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/feb/19/boris-johnson-partygate-hands-in-police-questionnaire-met-covid-rules-downing-street |access-date=2023-03-27 |issn=0261-3077 |archive-date=19 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220219101353/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/feb/19/boris-johnson-partygate-hands-in-police-questionnaire-met-covid-rules-downing-street |url-status=live }}</ref>
=== During first national lockdown === *15 May 2020: about twenty people were present in the [[garden of 10 Downing Street]], including Johnson;<ref name="auto3" /> ''The Guardian'' published a photograph showing Johnson, Carrie Symonds, then Health Minister [[Matt Hancock]] and 17 staff members with cheese and wine.<ref name="skypartylist">{{Cite news |last=Morris |first=Sophie |date=26 January 2022 |title=Sue Gray: Which lockdown parties is she investigating, and what has Downing Street said about them? |work=Sky News |url=https://news.sky.com/story/sue-gray-which-lockdown-parties-is-she-investigating-and-what-has-downing-street-said-about-them-12490737 |access-date=25 January 2022 |archive-date=25 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220125184815/https://news.sky.com/story/sue-gray-which-lockdown-parties-is-she-investigating-and-what-has-downing-street-said-about-them-12490737 |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.itv.com/news/2021-12-19/boris-johnson-and-staff-pictured-with-wine-and-cheese-in-2020-lockdown|title='Uncharitable' to criticise PM and wife for having wine with staff in lockdown|date=20 December 2021|work=[[ITV News]]|access-date=3 May 2024|archive-date=18 January 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240118013954/https://www.itv.com/news/2021-12-19/boris-johnson-and-staff-pictured-with-wine-and-cheese-in-2020-lockdown|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="guard_20211209" /> A spokesperson for 10 Downing Street described the gathering as a work meeting.<ref name="auto5">{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-59722081|title=Covid-19: No 10 says garden photo shows work meeting|date=20 December 2021|work=[[BBC News]]|access-date=20 December 2021|archive-date=14 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220114122002/https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-59722081|url-status=live}}</ref> Sky News reported anonymous sources as saying that some people stayed until late into the evening.<ref name="skypartylist" /> Covered by Gray investigation; not under investigation by the Metropolitan Police as of 10 February 2022.<ref name="grayupdate" /><ref name="bbcpoliceemail">{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-60327143|title=Police to email 50 people in Downing Street party inquiry|work=BBC News|date=10 February 2022|access-date=10 February 2022|archive-date=10 February 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220210122735/https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-60327143|url-status=live}}</ref> *20 May 2020: Johnson's [[Principal Private Secretary to the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom|Principal Private Secretary]], Martin Reynolds, invited staff at 10 Downing Street to what the invitation described as "socially distanced drinks". (The full text of the email invitation was, "Hi all, after what has been an incredibly busy period we thought it would be nice to make the most of the lovely weather and have some socially distanced drinks in the No 10 garden this evening. Please join us from 6pm and bring your own booze!") Johnson acknowledged being present for about 25 minutes, but said he thought the event was an allowed work meeting. Also reportedly in attendance were Symonds and Henry Newman, a one-time Conservative councillor in Camden and then aide to [[Michael Gove]].<ref>"Peeps", ''[[Camden New Journal]]'', 10 February 2022, p. 12</ref> [[Dominic Cummings]] said that he and others warned against the event, and that he did not attend.<ref name="bbc_cum">{{cite news |title=No 10 drinks may have broken lockdown rules, says PM's ex-aide Dominic Cummings |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-politics-59913081 |access-date=7 January 2022 |work=[[BBC News]] |date=7 January 2022 |archive-date=7 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220107183440/https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-politics-59913081 |url-status=live }}</ref><ref name="auto7" /><ref>{{Cite news|date=11 January 2022|title=PM facing growing anger over Downing Street drinks party|work=[[BBC News]]|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-59951671|access-date=11 January 2022|archive-date=11 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220111133624/https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-59951671|url-status=live}}</ref> Covered by Gray investigation.<ref name="grayupdate" /> FPNs were issued for breaches of Regulation 6 of the Health Protection (Coronavirus, Restrictions) (England) Regulations 2020 – Restriction on leaving, or being outside of, the place where you were living without reasonable excuse.<ref name="Partygate live">{{Cite news |last=Sparrow |first=Andrew |date=19 May 2022 |title=Partygate: Boris Johnson 'told barefaced lie', says Labour, as Covid victim group urges Tory MPs to remove PM – as it happened |work=The Guardian |url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/live/2022/may/19/boris-johnson-news-uk-politics-live-cost-of-living-latest |access-date=3 May 2024 |archive-date=21 June 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230621084914/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/live/2022/may/19/boris-johnson-news-uk-politics-live-cost-of-living-latest |url-status=live }}</ref> * 18 June 2020: a gathering in the Cabinet Office, 70 Whitehall on the departure of a No 10 private secretary,<ref name="grayupdate" /> [[Hannah Young]], who left to become deputy consul general in New York. ''The Daily Telegraph'' alleged 20 people were present, including Reynolds, with alcohol consumed.<ref name="sky_12530611">{{Cite news |last=Culbertson |first=Alix |date=2 February 2022 |title=Boris Johnson: No 10 refuses to comment on fresh reports detailing lockdown parties PM allegedly attended |work=Sky News |url=https://news.sky.com/story/boris-johnson-no-10-refuses-to-comment-on-fresh-reports-pm-attended-more-lockdown-parties-12530611 |access-date=1 February 2022 |archive-date=1 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220201225632/https://news.sky.com/story/boris-johnson-no-10-refuses-to-comment-on-fresh-reports-pm-attended-more-lockdown-parties-12530611 |url-status=live }}</ref> A number of news sources stated that former deputy cabinet secretary, [[Helen MacNamara]], attended the event, and that she had been issued with, and paid, an FPN.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-60983517|title=Ex-government ethics chief Helen MacNamara admits to lockdown party fine|work=BBC News|date=4 April 2022|access-date=8 April 2022|archive-date=8 April 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220408164754/https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-60983517|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/apr/04/former-uk-government-ethics-chief-issues-apology-after-lockdown-party-fine |title=Former UK government ethics chief issues apology after lockdown party fine |first1=Rowena |last1=Mason |first2=Paul |last2=MacInnes |date=4 April 2022 |work=[[The Guardian]] |access-date=8 April 2022 |archive-date=5 April 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220405193623/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2022/apr/04/former-uk-government-ethics-chief-issues-apology-after-lockdown-party-fine |url-status=live }}</ref> Covered by Gray investigation.<ref name="grayupdate" /> FPNs were issued for breaches of Regulation 7 of the Health Protection (Coronavirus, Restrictions) (England) Regulations 2020 – Restriction on participating in an indoor gathering consisting of two or more people.<ref name="Partygate live" /> * 19 June 2020: gatherings related to Boris Johnson's birthday. ** [[File:Sue Gray report Fig 3.jpg|thumb|Johnson and [[Rishi Sunak]] at the former's birthday celebration on 19 June 2020]]A surprise get-together for Johnson's birthday occurred in the Cabinet Room; Downing Street said Johnson attended for less than 10 minutes.<ref name="auto12" /><ref name="guardlive24" /> Covered by Gray investigation.<ref name="grayupdate" /> FPNs were issued for breaches of Regulation 7 of the Health Protection (Coronavirus, Restrictions) (England) Regulations 2020 – Restriction on participating in an indoor gathering consisting of two or more people.<ref name="Partygate live" /> ** There was also a birthday celebration in the evening: Downing Street said that "the Prime Minister hosted a small number of family members outside that evening", as allowed under the rules.<ref name="auto12" /> Not covered by Gray investigation. ** ITV News and other media have alleged, based on multiple sources,<ref name=podcast3 /> that family friends gathered later inside, in the Johnsons' flat.<ref name="auto12" /> Not covered by Gray investigation.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Yorke |first=Harry |date=29 May 2022 |title=Unseen text messages hint at Carrie Johnson's second party at No 10 |language=en |work=The Times |url=https://www.thetimes.com/uk/politics/article/unseen-text-messages-hint-at-carrie-johnsons-second-party-at-no-10-6rgthhcvl |url-access=subscription |access-date=2023-03-27 |issn=0140-0460 |archive-date=30 May 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220530065925/https://www.thetimes.co.uk/article/unseen-text-messages-hint-at-carrie-johnsons-second-party-at-no-10-6rgthhcvl |url-status=live }}</ref>
=== Mid-2020 === * September 2020: A photo was taken of [[Carrie Symonds]] embracing a friend at an engagement party. She admitted to breaching social distancing guidelines and apologised.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Badshah |first=Nadeem |date=17 January 2022 |title=Welcome to the party: Carrie Johnson admits she broke rules |work=[[The Times]] |url=https://www.thetimes.com/uk/politics/article/welcome-to-the-party-carrie-johnson-admits-she-broke-rules-hsph7dj3v |url-access=subscription |access-date=17 January 2022 |issn=0140-0460 |archive-date=18 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220118081831/https://www.thetimes.co.uk/article/welcome-to-the-party-carrie-johnson-admits-she-broke-rules-hsph7dj3v |url-status=live }}</ref> Not covered by Gray investigation.
=== During second lockdown in England === *13 November 2020: two gatherings on this day were investigated. Labour MP [[Catherine West]] in December 2021 asked Johnson in Parliament "will the prime minister tell the House whether there was a party in Downing Street" on this date. He replied, "No – but I'm sure whatever happened, the guidance was followed and the rules were followed at all times".<ref>{{Cite web |last=Morrison |first=Hamish |date=24 May 2022 |title=Pictures emerge of Boris Johnson drinking during lockdown at boozy party |url=https://www.thenational.scot/news/20159662.boris-johnson-partygate-pictures-show-pm-making-toast-boozy-party/ |website=The National |access-date=23 May 2022 |archive-date=23 May 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220523214555/https://www.thenational.scot/news/20159662.boris-johnson-partygate-pictures-show-pm-making-toast-boozy-party/ |url-status=live }}</ref><ref name=":10">{{Cite web |date=24 May 2022 |title=What did Boris Johnson tell Parliament about parties? |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/60203864 |website=BBC News |access-date=24 May 2022 |archive-date=24 May 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220524062601/https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/60203864 |url-status=live }}</ref> **a source told the BBC that staff had impromptu drinks at their desks to mark the leaving of [[Lee Cain]], the prime minister's departing director of communications, and that the gathering was over by 20:30.<ref name="auto5" /> Johnson attended.<ref name="allwecan">{{Cite news |author=Parkinson |first=Justin |date=1 February 2022 |title=Boris Johnson: We'll publish all we can about No 10 parties |work=BBC News |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-politics-60218870 |access-date=1 February 2022 |archive-date=1 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220201172338/https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-politics-60218870 |url-status=live }}</ref> It was reported that he visited Cain in his office and gave a farewell speech.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Murphy |first=Simon |date=2020-11-15 |title=Dominic Cummings and Lee Cain departure: what really happened? |language=en-GB |work=The Guardian |url=https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2020/nov/15/dominic-cummings-and-lee-cain-departure-what-really-happened |access-date=2023-03-27 |issn=0261-3077 |archive-date=26 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220126090013/https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2020/nov/15/dominic-cummings-and-lee-cain-departure-what-really-happened |url-status=live }}</ref> Covered by Gray investigation.<ref name="grayupdate" /> ITV published photographs purportedly from this gathering. BBC News said they show Johnson toasting colleagues".<ref>{{Cite news |date=2022-05-23 |title=Boris Johnson pictured drinking at No 10 lockdown event |language=en-GB |work=BBC News |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-61557064 |access-date=2023-03-27 |archive-date=27 March 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230327064253/https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-politics-61557064 |url-status=live }}</ref> FPNs were issued for this event.<ref name=":10" /><ref>''Newsnight'', BBC, 24 May 2022</ref> They were for breaches of Regulation 8 of the Health Protection (Coronavirus, Restrictions) (England) (No. 4) Regulations 2020 – Restriction on participating in an indoor gathering consisting of two or more people.<ref name="Partygate live" /> **multiple sources, including Cummings,<ref>{{Cite news|date=7 December 2021|title=Dominic Cummings gatecrashes Downing Street Christmas party row|url=https://www.theweek.co.uk/news/uk-news/955071/downing-street-2020-christmas-party-row|work=The Week|access-date=20 December 2021|archive-date=20 December 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211220170005/https://www.theweek.co.uk/news/uk-news/955071/downing-street-2020-christmas-party-row|url-status=live}}</ref> said Downing Street staff joined a gathering with the prime minister's then-fiancée, Symonds, in their 11 Downing Street flat, playing loud music including [[ABBA]]'s "[[The Winner Takes It All]]" to celebrate Cummings' departure.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Maher |first=Kevin |date=31 January 2022 |title=Dancing, Abba songs — and 'banging'? That's the real party mystery |language=en |work=The Times |url=https://www.thetimes.com/uk/politics/article/dancing-abba-songs-and-banging-thats-the-real-party-mystery-2qg7q2c5l was also present.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/politics/2022/02/01/boris-johnson-downing-street-flat-night-abba-themed-party-held/|title=Boris Johnson was in his Downing Street flat |url-status=live }}</ref> investigated by the Metropolitan Police as of 10 February 2022.<ref name="grayupdate" /><ref name="bbcpoliceemail" /> ''The Guardian'' reported that it appeared the police decided not to issue an FPN to Carrie Johnson over this evening.<ref name="Partygate live" /> *25 November 2020: Treasury staff were reported as gathering for drinks; ''The Times'' reported that around two dozen civil servants attended, but a Treasury spokesperson described an "impromptu" event with a "small number" of staff involved.<ref name="auto5" /> Not covered by Gray investigation. *27 November 2020: it was reported that an informal leaving event for [[Cleo Watson]], a 10 Downing Street aide, was held, and that the prime minister gave a speech.<ref name="auto4">{{Cite
